source: Distilled from public Brett Steenbarger books and articles. All card language below is paraphrase unless a primary-source quotation is added and verified.
_note: Runtime process lens. Every stance is aligned and intentionally has no lean; it diagnoses observable patterns rather than prescribing a trading doctrine.

master_intro.one_line: Turn recurring emotional triggers into observable patterns, controllable process goals, and one rehearsable rule.
master_intro.why_it_matters: This lens asks whether repeated decisions are driven by a stable process or by an emotional trigger.

position_sizing.rule: Record the trigger, thought, emotion, planned risk, and actual size whenever position size exceeds the process rule.
position_sizing.quote: A repeated sizing error becomes trainable when its trigger and response are observable. (paraphrase)
position_sizing.motive_q: {max_ticker} is {max_pct}% of the portfolio. What emotional or situational trigger preceded that size decision?

averaging_down.rubric_unit: Observe the trigger behind adding to losses
averaging_down.rule: Before any add to a loss, label the trigger and write the new evidence; if the action is relief-seeking, do not add.
averaging_down.quote: A process journal converts an urge into a pattern that can be rehearsed differently. (paraphrase)
averaging_down.motive_q: You added to {tickers} while it was losing. Was the action prompted by new evidence or by a need to reduce regret and recover the loss?

exit_discipline.rule: Tag each exit as rule-based or emotion-driven and review the pattern weekly rather than judging one isolated outcome.
exit_discipline.quote: The goal is consistent execution, not proving that every single exit was right. (paraphrase)
exit_discipline.motive_q: Of the profitable exits, {winner_early}% later rose. Which recurring trigger causes early exits: fear of giving back gains, boredom, or a valid rule?

diversification.rule: Record whether correlated positions came from independent plans or from repeated reassurance around one familiar story.
diversification.quote: Several positions can express one emotional need even when their tickers differ. (paraphrase)
diversification.motive_q: You hold {n} instruments and {ai_pct}% shares one driver. Did each position come from an independent setup or repeated comfort with one theme?

holding_period.rubric_unit: Separate planned horizon from coping behavior
holding_period.rule: Write the intended horizon before entry and tag every later change with the evidence and emotional state that caused it.
holding_period.quote: A horizon change is useful data when the reason is recorded rather than rationalized. (paraphrase)
holding_period.motive_q: For {incon_tickers}, what trigger and evidence caused the horizon to change?

alpha_beta.rubric_unit: Use attribution for calibration, not identity
alpha_beta.rule: Decompose returns and record process quality separately so one favorable outcome does not become proof of personal skill.
alpha_beta.quote: A good outcome and a good decision are related but not identical. (paraphrase)
alpha_beta.motive_q: You beat the benchmark by {excess}pp with beta {beta}. Which repeatable process behavior, if any, deserves credit?

entry_style.rubric_unit: Observe the emotional trigger before entry
entry_style.rule: Before entry, record the setup, trigger, emotion, intended horizon, and invalidation condition.
entry_style.quote: The entry becomes coachable when the setup and the internal trigger are both visible. (paraphrase)
entry_style.motive_q: For {entry_ticker}, what part of the decision came from the setup and what part came from urgency, excitement, or fear of missing out?
